area_description: Reasoning in LLMs encompasses techniques to elicit, train, verify, and enhance the logical and structured thinking capabilities of large language models, spanning prompting strategies, reinforcement learning, test-time compute scaling, reward models, and latent reasoning architectures.

categories.name: Reasoning Elicitation through Prompting
categories.description: Techniques that elicit reasoning from LLMs through prompt design, including chain-of-thought prompting, structured reasoning formats, and ensemble methods that improve answer reliability.

sub_topics.name: Chain-of-Thought Prompting
sub_topics.description: Prompting LLMs to produce intermediate reasoning steps before arriving at a final answer, including zero-shot and few-shot variants.

sub_topics.name: Structured Reasoning Prompts
sub_topics.description: Advanced prompting frameworks that organize reasoning into explicit structures such as trees, graphs, or multi-step plans.

sub_topics.name: Self-Consistency and Ensemble Methods
sub_topics.description: Generating multiple reasoning paths and aggregating answers via majority voting or other ensemble techniques to improve reliability.

categories.name: Training for Reasoning
categories.description: Methods that improve LLM reasoning through training, including reinforcement learning with reasoning rewards, supervised fine-tuning on reasoning traces, and distilling reasoning abilities from stronger models.

sub_topics.name: RL-based Reasoning Training
sub_topics.description: Using reinforcement learning to train models to produce better reasoning, including PPO, GRPO, and other RL algorithms applied to reasoning tasks.

sub_topics.name: SFT on Reasoning Traces
sub_topics.description: Supervised fine-tuning on curated reasoning traces to teach models to reason step-by-step during generation.

sub_topics.name: Reasoning Distillation
sub_topics.description: Transferring reasoning capabilities from larger or more capable models to smaller models through distillation of reasoning traces and strategies.

categories.name: Test-time Compute and Search
categories.description: Techniques that allocate additional computation at inference time to improve reasoning quality, including search-based methods, adaptive compute, and extended thinking.

sub_topics.name: Search-based Methods
sub_topics.description: Using tree search, beam search, or Monte Carlo Tree Search (MCTS) at inference time to explore reasoning paths and select the best solution.

sub_topics.name: Adaptive Compute
sub_topics.description: Dynamically adjusting the amount of computation based on problem difficulty, spending more tokens or inference steps on harder problems.

sub_topics.name: Extended Thinking
sub_topics.description: Allowing models to generate longer internal reasoning chains or 'thinking' tokens before producing a final answer.

categories.name: Reasoning Verification and Reward Models
categories.description: Methods for verifying the correctness of reasoning steps and training reward models that evaluate reasoning quality, including process-level and outcome-level supervision.

sub_topics.name: Process Reward Models
sub_topics.description: Reward models that evaluate the correctness of individual reasoning steps rather than just the final answer.

sub_topics.name: Outcome Reward Models
sub_topics.description: Reward models that evaluate reasoning based on the correctness of the final outcome or answer.

sub_topics.name: Self-Correction
sub_topics.description: Techniques enabling models to identify and fix errors in their own reasoning through iterative refinement.

sub_topics.name: Critic and Verifier Models
sub_topics.description: Separate models or components that act as critics or verifiers to evaluate and filter reasoning outputs.

categories.name: Latent and Non-verbal Reasoning
categories.description: Approaches that enable reasoning in continuous latent space rather than through explicit natural language tokens, including continuous thought representations and compressed reasoning.

sub_topics.name: Continuous Thought Representations
sub_topics.description: Reasoning in continuous embedding space rather than discrete token space, enabling richer and more efficient reasoning representations.

sub_topics.name: Recursive Layer Reasoning
sub_topics.description: Architectures that perform reasoning by recursively applying transformer layers, allowing variable-depth computation.

sub_topics.name: Compressed Reasoning
sub_topics.description: Methods that compress reasoning chains into fewer tokens or latent representations while preserving reasoning quality.

theme.name: Mathematical Reasoning
theme.description: Papers focused on mathematical problem solving, theorem proving, and numerical reasoning capabilities of LLMs.

theme.name: Logical Reasoning
theme.description: Papers addressing formal logic, deductive reasoning, inductive reasoning, and logical inference in LLMs.

theme.name: Commonsense Reasoning
theme.description: Papers on reasoning about everyday knowledge, physical intuition, and social understanding.

theme.name: Code Reasoning
theme.description: Papers on reasoning about code, program synthesis, debugging, and algorithmic problem solving.

theme.name: Causal Reasoning
theme.description: Papers on understanding cause-and-effect relationships, counterfactual reasoning, and causal inference with LLMs.

theme.name: Analysis
theme.description: Papers where experiments are conducted to evaluate the performance of baseline solutions, showing gaps and future directions.

theme.name: Benchmark
theme.description: Papers that introduce new benchmark datasets and evaluation metrics.

theme.name: Application
theme.description: Papers that show how to apply the techniques to a specific domain or task, highlighting its strengths and gaps.

theme.name: Survey
theme.description: Papers that provide a comprehensive overview of the current state of the art, including its taxonomy, advantages and limitations.
